summary: Added a new tooling script, `tools/rename_symbols.py`, that reads non-local symbols from `expected/build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.elf` and rewrites matching placeholder labels/functions in asm and source files. The script targets `.s`, `.c`, and `.h` files under `asm/` and `src/`, replacing names like `lbl_80000000` or `func_80000000` with symbol names from the ELF `.symtab` when available.
what_changed: Created `tools/rename_symbols.py` as a new 55-line Python utility.
The script opens `expected/build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.elf` with `pyelftools` and reads the `.symtab` section.
It filters out local symbols by excluding symbols whose bind is `STB_LOCAL`.
It also excludes a small hard-coded set of illegal or unwanted names: `_ctors`, `_dtors`, `_eti_init_info`, and `_SDA_BASE_`.
It builds an address-to-symbol-name dictionary using each symbol's `st_value` formatted as an 8-digit uppercase hexadecimal string.
It scans assembly and source targets from `asm/**/*.s`, `src/**/*.c`, and `src/**/*.h`.
It avoids generated-looking suffix combinations listed as `.s.s`, `.s.c`, and `.c.s` via a suffix filter, though the evidence only shows the exact code and not a review discussion of why.
It replaces word-boundary matches of `lbl_XXXXXXXX` or `func_XXXXXXXX` with the corresponding ELF symbol name when the address exists in the symbol table.
It prints each performed replacement in the form `old_name -> new_name` and writes files only when text changes.
smart_moves: Used the debug ELF as the source of truth instead of hand-maintaining large batches of renames.
Keyed symbols by address, which matches the existing decomp placeholder naming convention `lbl_XXXXXXXX` and `func_XXXXXXXX`.
Restricted replacements to placeholder names matching `lbl_` or `func_` plus exactly eight uppercase hex digits, reducing the risk of accidental broader text edits.
Skipped `STB_LOCAL` symbols, avoiding replacement with local/internal symbol names that may be less useful or potentially conflicting.
Maintained an explicit denylist for known special symbols that should not become ordinary source/asm names.
Resolved target file paths before processing and only rewrote files whose contents actually changed.
decomp_lessons: A small project-local symbol-renaming script can accelerate cleanup after debug symbols or expected ELF symbols become available.
For decomp projects that encode unknown functions and labels as address-based names, an address-to-symbol map from `.symtab` is a straightforward bridge from placeholders to real names.
When bulk-renaming identifiers, filter the symbol source and the replacement pattern narrowly; this PR only replaces `lbl_XXXXXXXX` and `func_XXXXXXXX` tokens and excludes local/special symbols.
Printing each rename gives a lightweight audit trail for bulk automated edits.
When automation touches both asm and C/header files, prefer a deterministic script in `tools/` so future contributors can rerun or inspect the same transformation.
naming_conventions: Unknown labels follow the placeholder form `lbl_XXXXXXXX`, where `XXXXXXXX` is an uppercase 8-digit address.
Unknown functions follow the placeholder form `func_XXXXXXXX`, where `XXXXXXXX` is an uppercase 8-digit address.
Replacement names are taken directly from ELF symbol names when a non-local, non-denylisted symbol has a matching `st_value` address.
Special ELF names `_ctors`, `_dtors`, `_eti_init_info`, and `_SDA_BASE_` are treated as illegal replacement names by this script.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Read symbols from the ELF `.symtab` section via `get_section_by_name(".symtab")` and `iter_symbols()`.
Computed lookup keys with `f'{symbol.entry.st_value:08X}'` so symbol addresses match the hexadecimal suffix in placeholder names.
Used the regex `\b(?:lbl|func)_(?P<addr>[\dA-F]{8})\b` to find only address-style placeholder identifiers.
Applied `re.sub` with a callback so each match could be looked up by address and logged when changed.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that modify or replace `tools/rename_symbols.py`.
Search for use of `rename_symbols.py` in contributor workflows or documentation.
Check whether later tooling generalizes symbol extraction from `main.elf` or handles lowercase hex placeholders.
Look for later review feedback around bulk renaming, illegal symbol denylists, or generated file exclusions.
